item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While temporal paradoxes and anomalies tend to mess me up trying to fully comprehend what is going on, I thoroughly enjoyed this!
But, I gotta ask, why The Office reference for the index title? lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really loved this, my only tiny gripe is the daevite priest and the culture he seems to represent appears fairly patriarchal… and that doesn’t really jive with what we know about the daevas

That being said, terrific to bring your temporal antics into the world of the Sarkic and daeva

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As someone who hadn't encountered Temporal Site-01 and everything related to Dr. Xyank, Regina and the others prior to reading this, I must admit that I was absolutely thrown for a loop (heh) until I realized the whole universe this entry exists within.

I found the entry and story itself incredibly well-made and clever and the present burst of temporal scips is totally up my alley. But I also found myself kind of lost on the first read-through with no prior knowledge of Loopy, Iso and such.

I'm going with a no-vote for now; I just spent a whooping three hours reading about Temporal Site-01 and connecting entries to fully grasp this scip and it's content (not hours wasted though).
With that in mind, I'm having a hard time deciding whether the inaccessibility is too high; or if it's exactly the teaser entry you'd want for the Temporal Site-01 universe.

All that being said, the writing - especially in the transcripts - is extremely well-crafted and realistic and the universe very engaging.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Daevites were utilized well in this skip, and it's about time the North Dakota Pyramid was used for an SCP. It's basically asking for it.
Also, I was glad to have helped with the critiquing of this in what little ways I did. I didn't expect both of our new SCPs would be neighbors!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It might've been a thing that Rome pushed them back into China. Like they had an empire from the Mediterranean to China, but Rome pushed them back far [Though, since Rome never occupied territory bordering China, they would've needed helpor something].

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This entry has a lot of good Daevite lore here. Although I don't quite understand why the priest wanted to stop Rome in particular. China would make more sense here, based on SCP-140's own addendum 140d, and your mention of the dates 250-200 BCE. Unless the Daeva branched out in that era, with one civilization being in Northeast China and another being in the Mediterranean.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Daevite priest’s log doesn’t interest me at all, but the temporal shenanigans in the first log, the inherent tragedy of the last log, and the gruesome nature of the Daevite machine itself more than compensate.

item.title: Daevites
item.content:encoded:
This heavily expanded Daevite lore in a lot of good ways. I'm going to have to keep re-reading this to keep finding out what I missed the last time!

Excellent stuff!! :D

Edit: I'm going to have to read more to find out about this Sgt. Russel thing.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Another tragic story in the life of Captain Regina Watts, wherein she struggles to contain a retrocausal anomaly and gains knowledge of her own death. Tragedy and Temporal anomalies often walk hand in hand in this business.

Image credit time! First is in Public domain and can be found here. Second is released under CC By 2.0 generic, though it has been altered by me. The original can be found here.

Special thanks to my reviewers Doctor Fullham, DrAkimoto, MalyceGraves, Sonderance, SharpEmbrace, Dr Moned, et. al.

Also thanks to Roget for the title!
